title: VRP Receipt Envelope v1
description: Vacation Rental Protocol verifiable booking record. A versioned receipt with a FLAT array of attestations (ADR 0010 D1). Recursive sub-receipts and selective disclosure are reserved v2 extension points (present-but-empty in v1); a v1 verifier never recurses into them.

vrp_receipt_version.description: Receipt envelope version. v1 verifiers accept exactly "1.0".

subject.description: What the receipt is about (e.g. property, stay window, offer id). Kept generic so the envelope is not locked to vacation rentals.

issuer.description: The node identity that assembled the receipt.

attestations.description: FLAT list of attestations (ADR 0010 D1). A new trust layer = a new entry; no central approval.

source.description: Where the verifying key is published for this attestation (e.g. a JWKS URL).

signature.description: The signed artifact as a compact JWS. v1 verifies the signature over the JWS bytes as received (ADR 0010 D5).

ref.description: Opaque correlator binding this attestation to an external object (offer id, transaction id, …).

valid_from.description: Start of the attestation validity window (ADR 0010 D2; mandatory per attestation).

valid_until.description: End of the attestation validity window (ADR 0010 D2; mandatory per attestation).

tlog.description: OPTIONAL transparency-log inclusion proof (ADR 0010 D3). A missing tlog is NOT a failure; a v1 verifier must not claim log-anchored properties for an attestation that lacks one.

sub_receipt.description: RESERVED v2 extension point (ADR 0010 D1). Recursive/chained receipts. A v1 verifier does not recurse into it.

disclosure.description: RESERVED v2 extension point (ADR 0010 D1). Selective-disclosure / ZK pointers. Not interpreted by a v1 verifier.
